div1.entryFree:
Bedlam, 1) a hospital for lunatics: to B. with him! H6B V, 1, 131. Tom o' B. (the usual name of a sort of vagabond beggars), Lr. I, 2, 148. Adjectively: B. beggars, Lr. II, 3, 14 (q. v.).
2) a lunatic: B., have done! John II, 183. get the B. to lead him, Lr. III, 7, 103. Adjectively: art thou b.? H5 V, 1, 20 (Pistol's speech). the b. brainsick duchess, H6B III, 1, 51. a b. and ambitious humour, H6B V, 1, 132.
